cards.claim_boundary: Algorithm cards are local reasoning and orchestration descriptions only. They do not prove correctness, safety, security, production readiness, scientific truth, mathematical proof, model understanding, or autonomous authority.
cards.flow: gate duration history -> median/p90 baseline -> latest drift ratio -> pressure classification -> bounded timeout recommendation -> cheaper next gate when pressure rises
cards.operator_use: Use before expensive validation to choose bounded timeouts and reduce wasted Codex/operator cycles.
cards.summary: This is a local predictive control loop. Prior runtimes become a lightweight forecast. The next run is compared against that forecast. If drift rises, the system recommends a bounded timeout, targeted test, or timing inspection before another expensive evolve. It is prediction by feedback, not model-weight learning. NEXUS learns here by preserving timing evidence, comparing new runs to that evidence, and making future orchestration less wasteful. Compact form: duration history -> baseline -> drift -> anomaly -> bounded recommendation.
cards.title: Predictive Gate Timing / Runtime Pressure Algorithm

cards.claim_boundary: Algorithm cards are local reasoning and orchestration descriptions only. They do not prove correctness, safety, security, production readiness, scientific truth, mathematical proof, model understanding, or autonomous authority.
cards.flow: gate timings -> median baseline -> p90 tail estimate -> latest runtime -> drift ratio -> pressure level
cards.operator_use: Use before expensive validation to choose bounded timeouts and reduce wasted Codex/operator cycles.
cards.summary: Runtime pressure treats time as evidence. A slow pass is still a signal: it may indicate larger scope, machine load, hidden waits, or a gate becoming brittle.
cards.title: Runtime Pressure Model

cards.claim_boundary: Algorithm cards are local reasoning and orchestration descriptions only. They do not prove correctness, safety, security, production readiness, scientific truth, mathematical proof, model understanding, or autonomous authority.
cards.flow: timeout = max(min_timeout, min(max_timeout, p90 * 1.5))
cards.operator_use: Use as a compact reasoning card for the related NEXUS lane.
cards.summary: Timeouts are bounded budgets, not authority expansion. The recommendation may raise or lower attention on a gate, but it cannot hide failures or skip required seals.
cards.title: Adaptive Timeout Budgeting

cards.claim_boundary: Algorithm cards are local reasoning and orchestration descriptions only. They do not prove correctness, safety, security, production readiness, scientific truth, mathematical proof, model understanding, or autonomous authority.
cards.flow: changed files + runtime pressure -> cheapest valid next gate -> final evolve remains required before commit
cards.operator_use: Use as a compact reasoning card for the related NEXUS lane.
cards.summary: If only docs changed, run docs/readme tests first. If only Electron changed, run Electron smoke first. If only Python changed, run targeted unit tests first. If runtime pressure is high, inspect predictive timing before repeating full evolve.
cards.title: Gate Selection Policy

cards.claim_boundary: Algorithm cards are local reasoning and orchestration descriptions only. They do not prove correctness, safety, security, production readiness, scientific truth, mathematical proof, model understanding, or autonomous authority.
cards.flow: passed gate + unchanged inputs -> certificate -> resume from active wound -> avoid rerunning green gates
cards.operator_use: Use as a compact reasoning card for the related NEXUS lane.
cards.summary: Passed gates are local certificates for the current inputs. If a later gate fails, resume from the failed gate unless a passed gate input changed.
cards.title: Certificate Resume Policy

cards.claim_boundary: Algorithm cards are local reasoning and orchestration descriptions only. They do not prove correctness, safety, security, production readiness, scientific truth, mathematical proof, model understanding, or autonomous authority.
cards.flow: predictive timing -> scope classification -> gate selection -> dry-run plan -> final evolve seal required
cards.operator_use: Use before expensive validation to choose bounded timeouts and reduce wasted Codex/operator cycles.
cards.summary: Predictive Evolve turns the runtime pressure model into a bounded planning envelope. It recommends the cheapest useful next gate for the current scope, records the plan as evidence, and keeps the authority line clear: it does not execute the plan, mutate the repo, or replace full evolve before commit.
cards.title: Predictive Evolve Planner Algorithm

Cortex tells NEXUS whether memory is indexed, bounded, and vector-current; predictive evolve tells NEXUS the cheapest next validation path; cards tell Codex which reusable reasoning patterns exist. The output is a recommendation-only next step and a benchmark trend row, never autonomous execution.
cards.title: Predictive Memory Orchestrator Algorithm

cards.summary: Breath Pulse is the fast vital-sign loop for NEXUS. It reads existing reports, classifies missing or stale surfaces, detects canonical versus runtime Git pressure, and emits one compact operating phase. It may orient the next AI or human pass, but it cannot execute, self-authorize, calibrate, mutate source, or replace final evolve.
cards.title: Breath Pulse Algorithm
